findings.title: Closeout-pack evidence artifacts not present in code diff
findings.detail: The review noted that CP00-020 evidence artifacts were not present yet. This is expected because the single read-only review precedes evidence finalization in the closeout workflow.

findings.title: Asymmetric length-pinning of policy arrays
findings.detail: The validator length-pins forbiddenMatterTraceClaims but not matterRequiredClaims or failClosedOn. Contract/policy/fixture equality checks still catch drift; this is defense-in-depth guidance only.

security_notes: Pure deterministic metadata-only precheck with no eval, SQL, shell, I/O, or state mutation.

Forbidden and unknown claim handling is fail-closed, including Matter bypass, fixture generation, fixture persistence, real-data, credential, secret, service execution, runtime route, and product-write claims.
Human approval boundaries are preserved by forbidden replace_human_approval and bypass_human_approval claims.
Fixture data is synthetic and carries no client, Matter, credential, or secret data.
test_notes: Unit coverage includes Matter-scoped allow, firm-level allow, Matter-required denial, forbidden claim rejection, module shape, and fixture parity.
RP00 validation wiring recomputes S02 to S03 to S04 live and checks fixture parity plus denied, bypass, and unknown-claim paths.
Pre-review evidence: syntax checks passed; node --test packages/control-plane/test/service.test.js passed 438/438; RP00 validator failed only for expected missing CP00-020 evidence files.
raw_review.summary: CP00-020 correctly adds a deterministic metadata-only Synthetic Fixture Set Matter trace precheck for RP00.P02.M06.S04. No P0/P1/P2 findings; two non-blocking P3 findings adjudicated.

raw_review_text:
# CP00-020 Read-Only Review — RP00.P02.M06.S04 Synthetic Fixture Set Matter trace precheck

**overall_verdict:** APPROVE
**blocks_pack_closeout:** false
**blocks_goal_closeout:** false
**production_ready_blocked:** false

**finding_counts:** P0: 0 · P1: 0 · P2: 0 · P3: 2

---

## Gate assessment

| Gate | Status | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| implementation | PASS | Policy, prechecker module, `precheckControlPlaneSyntheticFixtureSetMatterTrace`, getter, and both assert validators added (`policies.js`, `service.js`, `validators.js`). |
| tests | PASS | 7 new tests: policy binding, allow matter-scoped, allow firm-level, deny matter-required-without-matter (+ validator rejects emptied refs), 15-case fail-closed matrix, module shape, fixture mirror. |
| Hermes/RP00 validation wiring | PASS | `validate-rp00-control-plane-contract.mjs` adds weighted-ledger boundary, definition/policy/prechecker/fixture cross-checks, live S02→S03→S04 recompute, denied/rejection live checks, README + test text assertions; S03 added to `requiredClosedSubphases`, current advanced to S04. |
| no-real-data | PASS | `no_real_data: true` throughout; synthetic-only namespaces/domains; forbidden claims include `use_real_client_data`/`use_real_matter_data`/`use_credentials_or_secrets`. |
| no product-state writes | PASS | `writes_product_state: false`, all `does_not_*`/`*_permitted:false` flags, `Object.freeze` on result + nested arrays, pure function (no I/O). |
| no fixture generation/persistence | PASS | `fixture_generation_permitted`/`fixture_persistence_permitted: false`; forbidden `generate_fixture`/`persist_fixture`; `does_not_generate_fixture`/`does_not_persist_fixture: true`. |
| exactly one read-only review | PASS | This is the sole review; no other review artifact in diff. |
| adjudication | PASS (no blockers) | No P0–P2 findings to adjudicate; artifact is closeout-workflow scope, outside code diff. |
| construction inspection | PASS-with-note | Validation wiring added and consistent with S03; see F1 re: artifact/inline-flag scope. |
| included unit production_ready | PASS | `status: production_ready` consistent across contract current_subphase, subphase_closeouts entry, definition, policy, prechecker, fixture; `production_ready_requires` flag added. |
| commit | N/A | Working-tree diff; commit is post-review. |

---

## Findings

**[F1] P3 · blocking: false**
- location: `contracts/control-plane-contract.json` (subphase_closeouts S04) / `docs/closeout-packs/cp00-020/*`
- title: Closeout-pack evidence artifacts not present in code diff
- detail: The cp00-020 adjudication, construction-inspection, and command-evidence artifacts are not in this diff (expected — they are closeout-workflow scope). The added S04 `subphase_closeouts` entry carries no inline `construction_inspection.control_plane_synthetic_fixture_set_matter_trace_precheck_verified` flag, so the new validation check enforces only when an inline inspection exists — same pattern as prior closed subphases (S01–S03). Artifact existence cannot be confirmed from the diff alone; relies on the established process.

**[F2] P3 · blocking: false**
- location: `packages/control-plane/src/validators.js` (`assertControlPlaneSyntheticFixtureSetMatterTracePrecheckPolicy`)
- title: Asymmetric length-pinning of policy arrays
- detail: `forbiddenMatterTraceClaims` is length-pinned (`!== 19`) but `matterRequiredClaims` and `failClosedOn` are not. Drift is still caught downstream by `JSON.stringify` equality between contract definition, policy module, and fixture in the validation script — defense-in-depth asymmetry only.

---

## security_notes
No vulnerabilities introduced. Pure, deterministic, metadata-only boundary: no eval/SQL/shell/I/O, no state mutation (frozen result). Claim handling is correctly fail-closed — the `value !== undefined && !== null && !== false` gate treats any explicitly-asserted value (incl. `0`/`""`) as a claim, so forbidden/unknown claims reject while absence sentinels are safely ignored. Unknown own-property keys (incl. `__proto__`) hit the unknown-claim throw; no prototype pollution since claim keys are never used as assignment targets. Human-approval boundary preserved (`replace_human_approval`/`bypass_human_approval` forbidden). Fixture data is fully synthetic; carried `command_evidence_ref` points to a repo path (cp00-016), not client data.

## test_notes
Coverage is strong across allow (matter-scoped + firm-level), deny (matter-required-missing with negative validator assertion), and a broad fail-closed matrix. Note: matrix and denied-tenant-boundary cases use `assert.throws(fn, Error, label)` where the third arg is the failure message, not a matcher — they assert *that* an Error throws, not *which* guard fired (acceptable, slightly imprecise). Firm-level/deny tests depend on S03 permitting `matter_id: null`; logic is internally consistent but that upstream behavior is outside this diff. Live validation-script checks recompute the full S02→S03→S04 chain against the fixture and exercise denied/bypass/unknown-claim rejection.

## summary
Clean, well-scoped metadata-only precheck that correctly enforces Matter-first trace rules with fail-closed semantics, full no-write/no-real-data/no-fixture-generation guarantees, and comprehensive contract/policy/fixture/test cross-consistency. No blocking findings; two P3 informational notes (closeout-artifact diff scope, minor length-pin asymmetry). Approve for pack closeout at production_ready.
adjudication_notes: Claude returned APPROVE with no P0, P1, or P2 findings. Two P3 findings are adjudicated in adjudication.md.
